signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Macro Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7011 testing a critical breakdown level as the G10's primary laggard. USD outperformance (avg +0.714%) vs AUD weakness (avg -1.205%) creates a 1.919% spread, the widest in the G10 complex. Despite a -72bp OIS carry advantage for AUD, the pair is pressured by negative economic surprises (CESI -8) and headline risk ahead of US CPI. A core CPI print above 3% accelerates the breakdown toward 0.6950. COT LF net long +56,800 (76.5%) remains a significant liquidation risk if price fails to hold 0.7000.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.47 holding above the 160.00 psychological handle, a level previously associated with BoJ verbal intervention. The +290bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.73%) continues to support the bid despite a moderate options put skew (25d RR -1.10). Retail sentiment is 73% short, diverging from the institutional carry-driven trend. Sustained trading above 160.00 increases the probability of direct BoJ action, though CESI (+17) suggests JPY fundamental resilience is improving.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Options Skew
signals.text: GBP/USD IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 6.5% generates a +4.5% Vol Risk Premium, the highest in the G10. This elevated premium combined with a moderate put skew (25d RR -0.78) indicates the market is paying a significant premium for downside protection ahead of the June 18 BoE meeting. While OIS carry is neutral (-10bp), the real rate differential (+0.77% UK vs +1.23% US) favors USD. Watch for a break of 1.3350 to confirm the options market's bearish bias.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Retail vs COT Positioning Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5813 exhibits a sharp positioning conflict: retail traders are 88% long (extreme contrarian sell signal) while COT LF specs are net short -27,797 (long% 15.1%). This alignment of institutional shorts and retail longs typically precedes further downside extension. The -0.83% real policy rate (most accommodative in G10) provides no structural support against the +150bp USD carry advantage. Invalidation of the bearish bias requires a recovery above 0.5900.

signals.title: DXY vs SPX — Correlation Break + Funding Stress
signals.text: The correlation between DXY and S&P 500 has shifted to -0.348 (z=+1.66σ from 252d norm), signaling a move toward USD funding stress dynamics. As VIX rises to 21.3 (elevated volatility), the USD is attracting a risk-off bid that deviates from its standard equity-inverse relationship. This regime shift suggests USD strength may persist even if US yields soften, as liquidity preference dominates. Watch VIX 25.0 for a transition to full risk-off JPY/CHF outperformance.
